title: User Management
body_text:
User Management
1	Introduction to User Management
1.1	Overview of the Client Centre and User Management
The Client Centre is an electronic gateway that provides secured access to Central 1âs applications and to news and information about our products and services. All users must be set up with a Client Centre user ID and must register a username and password at their first login.
There are two Client Centre user types: User Management Security Officers (Security Officers) and Client Centre Users. Both user types access the Client Centre to log in to applications. Security Officers are also responsible for creating Client Centre Users, modifying, and deleting Client Centre users, and for authorizing changes to usersâ information, statuses, and/or access rights. Some applications are also accessed by your business customers, but not through the Client Centre (e.g.,
PaymentStreamâ¢
AFT and Corporate Capture). Security Officers also create and manage these users.
Note: 	Some applications also have a separate user management component. Users may require set-up on both the User Management application and the individual application. See Chapter 8, Managing Application Access and Permissions, for specific application requirements.
Most applications on the Client Centre are accessed via password login. However, a 2-step security token is also required to access certain applications (e.g., User Management) or to access certain functions within an application (e.g., pending incoming/outgoing wires in the Wires application).
This Part also includes procedures for assigning and managing 2-step security tokens. For general information on 2-step security, see the 2-Step Security Part.
Note:	Only Security Officers can access User Management.
